key_insight: Neural network depth is adapted by treating training as an optimal control problem and inserting new layers where a posteriori error estimates are largest.
engineering_relevance: This gives a principled way to refine network architectures for scientific machine learning, improving generalization while focusing compute on the most error-prone parts of the model.
method: Formulate network weights and biases as continuous-time control variables across depth.
Use dual weighted residual a posteriori error estimation to bound functional approximation error.
Insert new layers at interval locations with the largest estimated error contribution.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experiments on scientific datasets, including learning the observable-to-parameter map for Navier-Stokes, where the method consistently outperforms existing architecture adaptation approaches.
watch_item: The approach adds theoretical and implementation complexity because it requires reliable error estimation and careful placement of new layers during training.
paper_title: An optimal control approach for neural network architecture adaptation with a posteriori error estimation
